Cross Defendant Matagrano Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Cross Complainants Serve Well Market Inc And Thamin Najjars First Amended Cross Complaint For Failure To Timely Amend
Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT MATAGRANO, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Cross Complainants Serve Well Market Inc And Thamin Najjar's First Amended Cross Complaint For Failure To Timely Amend

Cross-defendant Matagrano, Inc.'s motion to dismiss first amended cross-complaint is denied. California has "a strong public policy favoring disposition on the merits." (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 398.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2020 Continued To Mar-25-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cherie J. Edson, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH, T:415.362.2580 for cross-defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Charles Joshua Katz, Esq. for cross-complainant/opposing party.

RULING: After a brief argument, cross complainan's counsel submits to the tentative ruling. Therefore, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT MATAGRANO, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-defendant Matagrano, Inc.'s demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The form cross-complaint pleads comparative negligence as to plaintiff Cesar Serrano. However, Serrano's complaint does not sue for negligence; instead it pleads employment claims and failure to purchase workers compensation insurance, by which emotional distress was inflicted. The cross-complaint and complaint give no indication how Matagrano could be jointly liable on the claims Serrano has pled.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that repeats the Court's tentative ruling verbatim, and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
